title: Is Rei Q a disposable battery
display_title: Is Rei Q a disposable battery
wikitext:
[]: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Thu Feb 11, 2016 7:04 am As I'm sitting here watching the much awaited and delayed release of 3.33 a thought has come to mind about Rei Q and her disposed clones. While she is bathing in the LCL tube just before Fuyutsuki invites Shinji to shogi, the power is restored. Anyone think this might explain the accumulation of Rei heads as they're nothing more than batteries for the reactor powering Nerv. If anyone has posted a similar topic please inform me as I haven't been here in god knows how long.

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Feb 11, 2016 1:38 pm I thought the power outage was a pretext for Fuyutsuki and Shinji to have a private conversation before the former went into an in-depth analysis of Rei and Yui's connection.

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 11, 2016 1:52 pm I assume that since a large, automated facility like Neo-Nerv HQ in inhabited by only five individuals, they don't keep the lights on in every room all the time. There might not even be active power in entire sections of the base. Maybe a generator needed time to boot up in order to restore power to the room Fuyu and Shinji had their conversation in. The accumulation of heads reads "spare parts" to me. I'm surprised there weren't any arms or legs sticking out either.

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Thu Feb 11, 2016 2:02 pm Sachi wrote: The accumulation of heads reads "spare parts" to me. I'm surprised there weren't any arms or legs sticking out either.

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Feb 11, 2016 2:18 pm I really like that idea, and from the top of my head there's nothing I could hold against this thesis. I also like the idea that Fuyutsuki did on purpose to have time to talk to Shinji. I for myself never thought much about the power outage. I thought it was just a way to generate dramatic tension, and make it seem more like a stage play, which it certainly did. But if there's also an in-universe reason for it, so much the better.

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Thu Feb 11, 2016 10:44 pm Interesting theory. In NGE Rei has enough power to project an AT field, so it's not that far-fetched to imagine an improved version being powerful enough to supply the geofront with electricity. The real question is, what parts of Rei Q do you need to press to check her charge level?

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Feb 12, 2016 4:11 am IronEvangelion wrote: Interesting theory. In NGE Rei has enough power to project an AT field, so it's not that far-fetched to imagine an improved version being powerful enough to supply the geofront with electricity. The real question is, what parts of Rei Q do you need to press to check her charge level?

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Feb 13, 2016 2:17 am Sachi wrote: Based upon abilities displayed, I'd say Rei III of NGE is more powerful than Rei Q. Especially considering that Rei Q doesn't seem to have the same soul at all as Rei II (whose soul is still trapped within Eva Unit-01), whereas Rei III of NGE's soul is a conglomerate of her previous iterations.

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Feb 14, 2016 9:04 am Alternate theory: Gendo was monitoring the conversation (after all, making Shinji go pas the Despair Horizon is vital to the Plan) and Fuyustuki made it so the power outage would happen to so the surveillance system would reset and give him some time to have a real private chat with Shinji. (maybe Gendo didn't wanted that Fuyutsuki mentions that Rei was a clone of Yui)

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sun Feb 14, 2016 8:48 pm ElMariachi wrote: Alternate theory: Gendo was monitoring the conversation (after all, making Shinji go pas the Despair Horizon is vital to the Plan) and Fuyustuki made it so the power outage would happen to so the surveillance system would reset and give him some time to have a real private chat with Shinji. (maybe Gendo didn't wanted that Fuyutsuki mentions that Rei was a clone of Yui)

[]: Re: Is Rei Q a disposable battery?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Mon Feb 15, 2016 5:14 am wiser3754 wrote: and the disclosure with Shinji about his mother, Rei and his father was part of "what you (Yui) wanted". as the information Shinji recieves leads him down the path of dispair.
